name: Sydney Metro West
desc: 24km twin-tunnel metro: Westmead → Parramatta → SOP → Five Dock → Burwood North → Strathfield → The Bays → Pyrmont → Hunter St CBD. 9 new stations. TBMs launched 2024. Cost revised from $20B → ~$25B+. Transforms Five Dock, Strathfield, Olympic Park — suburbs with zero metro access today. Largest active infrastructure project in Australia.
analogue: Metro C&SW — 18 months late, 12% over. Metro NW — on time, on budget. Average Sydney metro overrun ~15-20%. Twin tunnel under complex geology increases risk.

name: Sydney Metro — WSA Airport & Aerotropolis
desc: St Marys → Orchard Hills → Luddenham → WSA Airport → Aerotropolis/Bradfield. Stage 1 funding deed not signed. Stage 2 (Airport → Leppington) unfunded. Without metro, WSA cannot compete with Kingsford Smith for full-service international carriers. Aerotropolis housing growth of 100k+ dwellings depends on it.
analogue: Melbourne Airport Rail — defunded twice, rebid three times over 15 years. Aus airport metros have ~40% cancel/defer rate. Federal election outcomes are primary risk factor.

name: Western Sydney Airport (Nancy-Bird Walton)
desc: Greenfield international airport at Badgerys Creek (2555). Single 3,700m runway initially, terminal well advanced. Opens mid-2026. Committed by both major parties over multiple terms. Anchor demand driver for 100,000+ dwelling Aerotropolis growth area. First airlines: Qantas regional, Virgin, Bonza.
analogue: Perth Airport expansion — on schedule and budget. Greenfield airports with bipartisan multi-term commitment have near-100% proceed rates.

name: Sydney Metro — City & Southwest (Bankstown)
desc: Metro conversion: Sydenham → Bankstown. 10 stations. Final testing complete. Opening late 2025. Canterbury-Bankstown LGA — Australia's fastest-growing — gets metro-quality service. Directly enables TOD rezonings for 15,000+ dwellings at Campsie and Bankstown stations.
analogue: Metro Northwest — on time, on budget. Same delivery model and procurement approach.

name: WestConnex — M8 & Rozelle Interchange
desc: M8 (Kingsgrove → St Peters) and M4-M5 Link / Rozelle Interchange — operational 2023. Reduced Parramatta Rd traffic 40%+. Unlocked inner-west residential demand. Stage 3B Iron Cove Link under planning review — route through Drummoyne/Five Dock area.
analogue: Delivered. Australia's most complex urban motorway project completed. PPP model worked.

name: Western Harbour Tunnel
desc: Twin 6.5km tunnels under Sydney Harbour: Rozelle → North Sydney. Under construction. Cost $16B → $22B+. Second harbour road crossing. Beaches Link extension (Warringah Freeway → Balgowlah) under review — likely deferred. Critical for North Shore CBD employment catchment which currently has single-point-of-failure at Harbour Bridge/Tunnel.
analogue: NorthConnex ($3B) delivered on time. WHT is 7× larger with more complex harbour geology. PPP structure means cancellation risk low despite overrun.

name: F6 Extension Stage 2 — Kogarah to Loftus
desc: Tunnel through Sutherland Shire: Hurstville → Miranda → Loftus. Business case incomplete. Strong community opposition in Sutherland LGA. Federal funding uncommitted. Independent MPs in Cook and Heathcote both opposed. Stage 1 (Arncliffe → Beverly Hills) operational — Stage 2 entirely separate decision.
analogue: East West Link VIC — ALP cancelled after $1.2B spent. Exact same pattern: near-suburban tunnel, election-dependent, resistant community, reluctant federal partner.

name: WSA Airport Road Network
desc: Northern Road duplication, Bringelly Road, Elizabeth Drive. Multiple packages on schedule. Critical airport access before metro arrives — without roads, airport cannot open operationally.
analogue: Gold Coast CG airport roads — on schedule. Airport-essential access roads near-100% proceed.

name: M9 Western Sydney Orbital
desc: ~50km orbital freeway: M7 Eastern Creek → Blacktown → Marsden Park → Schofields → WSA Aerotropolis. Critical freight and worker access. Route evaluation underway — no EIS. Four marginal seats in corridor create political momentum but long timeline.
analogue: M7 Westlink — on time via Transurban PPP. Orbital motorways in outer Sydney have strong delivery track record when bipartisan committed.

name: WestConnex Stage 3B — Iron Cove Link
desc: Proposed tunnel from Rozelle Interchange (Iron Cove) to Drummoyne connecting to Five Dock. Completes WestConnex network. Strong opposition from Balmain/Drummoyne communities and Greens/IND politicians. No EIS yet. Route feasibility disputed.
analogue: WestConnex Stages 1-3 — delivered despite opposition. Iron Cove politically much harder: Balmain is now Greens-held, not ALP.

name: South West Rail Link Extension — Leppington to Macarthur
desc: Rail extension: Leppington → Oran Park → Narellan → Campbelltown/Macarthur. Opens up massive south-west growth corridor. Oran Park, Narellan have 60,000+ planned dwellings with no rail. Bipartisan intent but no funding committed.
analogue: South West Rail Link (Glenfield → Leppington) — delivered on time. Extension is the natural next stage but funding gap is the binding constraint.

name: Parramatta Light Rail Stage 2
desc: Camellia → Ermington → Wentworth Point → Sydney Olympic Park. Completes PLR loop. Not yet in NSW Budget. Wentworth Point has 20,000 residents currently isolated by bridge. Camellia TOD is stalled without this connection.
analogue: PLR Stage 1 — on budget Nov 2023. Stage 2 funding dependent on NSW fiscal position post Metro West overruns.

name: Greater Sydney TOD Rezoning — 37 Precincts
desc: 170,000+ dwellings across 37 station precincts: Crows Nest, St Leonards, Macquarie Park, Homebush, Rhodes, Meadowbank, Campsie, Bankstown, Parramatta, Penrith. NSW using SEPP override powers after 35+ councils challenged in LEC. SEPP override untested at scale.
analogue: Melbourne TODs — partial delivery. NSW SEPP override mechanism new — legal durability untested.

name: Blacktown & Mt Druitt Social Housing
desc: LAHC renewal and private development across Blacktown LGA (2148). Greenway is the most marginal federal seat in NSW. Extremely high political incentive to deliver. Strong rental demand from WSA aviation workers beginning to show.
analogue: Ivanhoe Estate (Narrabeen) — delivered. Public-private LAHC model works when properly resourced.

name: Ryde / Meadowbank Precinct
desc: Meadowbank station precinct: 10,000 dwellings, school, 4ha waterfront park. Ryde Council (LNP) challenging density in LEC. Ryde State seat = bellwether — won by government side in every election since 1988. Waterfront location on Parramatta River a major amenity premium.
analogue: Schofields Town Centre — delivered by SOPA. Meadowbank has better existing rail access.

name: NSW Social & Affordable Housing Fund
desc: 8,000+ social/affordable dwellings. Delivery via LAHC, Landcom, CHPs and private developers. Build-to-Rent incentives slow to activate. Distributed across Greater Sydney with western Sydney concentration.
analogue: NSW FACS 2018 — partial delivery. Delivery agency capacity the binding constraint.

name: Crows Nest / St Leonards TOD
desc: 6,800 dwellings within 800m of Crows Nest Metro station (2065). High-rise zones 30-40 storeys. Metro opened Aug 2024. North Sydney Council overridden. Strong demand from CBD workers — one of Sydney's most undersupplied premium suburbs.
analogue: Macquarie Park TOD — major towers built within 5 years of metro opening. Crows Nest has stronger fundamentals.

name: Bankstown / Campsie TOD
desc: 15,000+ dwellings at Bankstown (2200) and Campsie (2194) precincts. Cumberland Council challenging in LEC. Metro opens late 2025 directly enabling this. Canterbury-Bankstown has Australia's highest household formation rate — demand fundamentals exceptional.
analogue: Green Square TOD — rezoned 2010, 40,000 residents now in delivery. Bankstown has cheaper land and stronger demand uplift potential.

name: Penrith / Aerotropolis Housing Growth
desc: Major growth corridor: Penrith (2750) → Bradfield → Mamre Road → Kemps Creek (2765). 100,000+ dwellings over 20 years. Metro is essential — without rail, development slower and car-dependent. Lindsay is the federal bellwether seat for outer-western Sydney.
analogue: Schofields — delivered but road-first, infrastructure-lagging. Aerotropolis has better planning but same risk without metro.

name: Eraring Power Station Extension
desc: 2880MW Eraring (Australia's largest coal plant) extended to Aug 2027 via NSW Govt support deed. Further extension to 2029 under negotiation. Critical baseload buffer — no credible replacement baseload capacity before 2028. Origin seeking ongoing government underwriting.
analogue: Liddell managed via Govt pressure. Eraring harder — Origin is ASX-listed with shareholder obligations.

name: Illawarra Offshore Wind Zone
desc: Declared offshore wind zone off Wollongong. Proponents: Star of the South, BlueFloat, UPC/AC Renewables. No FID reached. Fishing industry, visual amenity, grid connection all unresolved. 5-10 year lead time from FID to first power is international industry standard.
analogue: No Australian offshore wind built. Gippsland (VIC) more advanced but still no FID. UK: 7 years average from consent to generation.

name: Hunter Renewable Energy Zone
desc: Hunter-New England REZ — 3,000MW+ wind, solar, batteries. HumeLink transmission upgrade is critical path item. Major coal-region economic transition. Without HumeLink, new generators cannot connect to the grid.
analogue: Central-West Orana REZ — progressing but behind. Transmission always bottleneck in NSW REZs.

name: Snowy 2.0 Pumped Hydro
desc: 2000MW pumped hydro storage — $2B (2017 estimate) → $12B+ now. TBM 'Kirra' stuck in fractured rock for 2+ years. Revised delivery 2028. Bipartisan committed — too embedded to cancel. Critical eastern grid storage. Political economy: too-big-to-fail applies absolutely.
analogue: No Australian precedent at this overrun scale. Closest: Sydney Opera House 1,357% over. Both became national icons despite costs.

name: EnergyConnect — NSW/SA Interconnector
desc: 900km HVDC/AC interconnector: NSW ↔ SA grids. Enables renewable transfer. Transgrid + ElectraNet joint delivery. Critical for grid stability as coal exits NSW market.
analogue: Basslink — delivered. HVDC interconnectors with regulatory backing have reasonable track record.

name: Sydney Desalination Plant Expansion
desc: Kurnell desal: 250ML/day → 500ML/day. EIS underway. Essential as Warragamba dam reliability declines. Proven technology — Veolia/SUEZ experienced contractors. Lowest delivery risk of any major NSW infrastructure project.
analogue: Perth Desal 2 — on schedule and budget. Sydney Desal Stage 1 (2010) within budget. Technology risk near-zero.

name: Greater Sydney Water Security
desc: Integrated water security: recycled water, stormwater harvesting, demand management. Multiple delivery authorities (WaterNSW, Sydney Water, councils). Warragamba spillway raise is contested separate component.
analogue: SEQ water grid — benchmark delivery. NSW package less coordinated but individually lower risk.

name: Warragamba Dam Spillway Raise
desc: 7m raise of Warragamba Dam wall to reduce flood risk for 134,000 people in Hawkesbury-Nepean. EIS rejected and resubmitted (2024). World Heritage Blue Mountains and Aboriginal cultural heritage impacts unresolved. If government changes at 2027 state election, this is cancelled.
analogue: Tillegra Dam (Hunter) — approved then cancelled after change of govt. Identical political trajectory.

name: Tech Central / Atlassian HQ
desc: 20ha innovation precinct (2000-2007). Atlassian HQ 40-floor net-zero tower under construction. 25,000 tech jobs targeted. Metro West Hunter St station adjacent — opening 2030. Google, Canva, Afterpay already expanding into precinct. Transforming Ultimo/Surry Hills into Sydney's tech quarter.
analogue: Fishermans Bend MEL — slower uptake. Atlassian anchor + Metro adjacency = meaningfully stronger demand signal.

name: Port Botany Terminal Expansion
desc: Additional berths, terminal automation, rail freight upgrades at Port Botany (2036). MUA industrial relations risk on automation. Rail freight link to western Sydney freight hub via Sydney Gateway Road.
analogue: Port of Melbourne — delivered. Port expansions with committed operators have high proceed rates.

name: Barangaroo Precinct
desc: $6B waterfront precinct — 30,000 workers, Crown casino, International Towers. Final residential buildings completing 2026. Australia's most successful large-scale waterfront renewal. Documented price premium in surrounding suburbs (Millers Point, Pyrmont, Dawes Point) of 15-25%.
analogue: Itself — delivered. Benchmark for precinct renewal nationally.

name: Sydney Olympic Park Renewal
desc: 40,000 residents at SOP (2127) by 2030. Metro West station transforms 40-min train to 10-min metro. New schools, parks, retail. SOPA as master developer. PLR Stage 1 already serving. Combined with Metro West = strongest multi-project investment case in NSW.
analogue: Wentworth Point — SOPA master developer, delivered. Same model at larger scale.

name: Sydney CBD Cycleway Network
desc: Protected cycleway expansion: Bourke St, Pitt St, Pyrmont, Alexandria. Rolling programme. Documented 25%+ mode shift on protected-lane corridors. Inner-city properties within 200m of protected cycleways showing measurable rental premium.
analogue: Melbourne CBD cycleways — usage exceeds forecasts every year. Lowest-risk infrastructure type.

name: Parramatta Square & Civic Precinct
desc: 6-tower commercial/civic precinct (2150). 50,000 workers, new library, council chambers. Anchors Parramatta CBD transformation. Walter Burley Griffin-inspired urban design. Private developer Walker Corp led delivery.
analogue: Delivered. Exemplar of private-led CBD renewal with council land contribution.

name: Camellia Remediation & TOD
desc: Remediation of contaminated industrial land at Camellia (2142) for 10,000-dwelling TOD. EPA-supervised. Adjacent to PLR Stage 1 and future Metro West. Contamination complexity key constraint.
analogue: Homebush Bay remediation (Olympic site) — delivered at 3x original cost estimate. Camellia has similar contamination profile.

name: Northern Beaches Hospital Precinct
desc: Frenchs Forest hospital (2086) operational — expansion underway. 5,000 staff at full capacity. Major employment anchor for Northern Beaches. Strongest demand driver for future Northern Beaches transit.
analogue: Sutherland Hospital expansion — delivered. Health PPPs have strong track record in NSW.

name: Liverpool Health & Innovation Precinct
desc: Liverpool Hospital (2170) expansion + innovation precinct. 2,000 additional jobs. Anchor for south-west Sydney economic transformation. Adjacent to T2 line.
analogue: Westmead Health Precinct — delivered. South-west Sydney's equivalent.

name: Macquarie Park Innovation District
desc: Macquarie Park (2113) biomedical and tech precinct. Anchor tenants: Macquarie University, Cochlear, Optus, Siemens. Metro Northwest directly serves. Major TOD adjacent adds density.
analogue: North Ryde tech precinct — delivered progressively. Macquarie Park is NSW's largest suburban employment cluster.

name: Bradfield Central Park
desc: 220-hectare central park at Bradfield Aerotropolis (2763). Will be NSW's largest new public domain in 30 years. Essential amenity to attract residents to new city.
analogue: Western Sydney Parklands — delivered. Public domain investment precedes private development by design.

name: Prospect Water Recycling Plant
desc: Prospect (2148) recycled water facility — 80ML/day capacity. Services Penrith and Aerotropolis non-potable needs. Reduces Warragamba dam dependence.
analogue: Rouse Hill recycled water — delivered. Proven technology, Sydney Water delivery record strong.

name: Sydney Fish Market (Completed)
desc: New Sydney Fish Market (2009) opened 2025. Adjacent to Metro West The Bays precinct. Working market + public domain. Catalysing Bays Precinct urban renewal.
analogue: Delivered. Modest overrun (~$65M) managed within contingency.

name: Green Square Public Domain
desc: Green Square Town Centre (2017) — new library, aquatic centre, plaza. Services 40,000 residents in Green Square TOD. Exemplar of council-led high-density infill infrastructure.
analogue: Delivered. Set benchmark for TOD public domain delivery.

name: Mulgoa Road Upgrade
desc: Stage 1+2 duplication of Mulgoa Road (2750) — connecting Penrith CBD to M4. Supports residential growth in St Marys, Kingswood, Penrith South. High use by Airport workers once WSA opens.
analogue: Great Western Highway upgrades — delivered in stages. Arterial upgrades have strong delivery record.

name: Hornsby Precinct Revitalisation
desc: Hornsby Town Centre (2077) TOD — 3,500 new dwellings at existing rail interchange. Hornsby Council cooperative on rezoning. Strong demand from upper North Shore families wanting density with amenity.
analogue: Chatswood TOD — delivered over 20 years. Hornsby following similar trajectory with better council relations.

name: Western Sydney University Campuses
desc: Expansion of WSU vertical campuses at Parramatta (2150), Liverpool (2170), Bankstown (2200). Major student catchment supports rental demand. Campus-adjacent density TOD follow-on likely.
analogue: RMIT/Swinburne vertical campuses Melbourne — delivered. Urban university expansion trend established.

name: Sydney Metro — Tallawong to St Marys (Northern Extension)
desc: 20km of new track linking Sydney Metro NW (Tallawong) to Sydney Metro WSA (St Marys) via Schofields, Marsden Park. 6-7 stations proposed. $40M business case funded in 2024-25 NSW Budget. Completes the metro loop around outer Western Sydney — connects North-West growth corridor to airport in one ride. Critical for Aerotropolis labour market access. No commitment to fund construction yet.
analogue: Melbourne Suburban Rail Loop — business case to construction took 4 years. Sydney metro extensions typically 6-8 year timeline from business case to opening.

name: Sydney Metro — Westmead to Aerotropolis (Metro West Extension)
desc: 37km extension of Sydney Metro West from Westmead through Sydney's western suburbs to the Aerotropolis/Bradfield. 6-8 stations proposed. Would create one-seat metro ride from Sydney CBD to airport via Parramatta. Business case underway alongside Tallawong-St Marys. Highest-impact post-2032 metro project.
analogue: Stage 1 Metro West cost ~$25B for 24km; this 37km extension faces similar TBM cost pressures + rougher geology in western Sydney basin.

name: Sydney Metro — Bankstown to Liverpool to Glenfield
desc: 20km extension from Bankstown (T3 metro terminus from 2026) through Liverpool to Glenfield (T2/T8 interchange). Connects Australia's largest LGA (Canterbury-Bankstown) to south-west growth corridor. Would also enable potential future link to Aerotropolis via Glenfield-Bradfield business case.
analogue: Similar suburb-to-suburb metro extensions in Melbourne (Suburban Rail Loop) priced at ~$600m/km — this 20km segment ~$12B realistic.

name: Sydney Metro — Glenfield to Aerotropolis (M9 Loop)
desc: Connects Glenfield (T2/T8 interchange) to Aerotropolis/Bradfield. ~25km. Federal Government has committed funding to business case. Combined with the Macarthur extension, would create south-west metro corridor. Federal funding signals higher delivery probability than other proposed extensions.
analogue: Federal funding commitments at business case stage have historically converted to construction within 5-7 years (e.g. WSA Stage 1, Suburban Rail Loop).

name: Sydney Metro — Macarthur to Aerotropolis
desc: 23km from Macarthur (T2/T8 terminus, Campbelltown) to Aerotropolis. 6-7 station locations. Connects 250k+ Macarthur residents to airport without the long T8 to Glenfield then bus journey. Federal Government funded the business case alongside Glenfield-Aerotropolis.
analogue: Macarthur is the highest-population area in Sydney without metro access; political pressure is significant.

name: M6 Extension Stage 1 — Arncliffe to Kogarah
desc: Twin 4km tunnels linking M8 Motorway at Arncliffe to President Avenue at Kogarah. Removes ~2,000 trucks/day from local roads, bypasses 23 sets of traffic lights on Princes Highway. Tunnel boring active. Stage 2 (Kogarah → Loftus) is project #6. Operational target 2028.
analogue: NorthConnex tunnel — delivered on budget, 6 months late. M6 Stage 1 expected similar profile.

name: M12 Motorway — M7 to Western Sydney Airport
desc: 16km toll-free motorway: M7 at Cecil Hills → Western Sydney Airport. Direct motorway connection to new airport. 4-lanes opening (provision for 6). Construction well advanced. Critical airport access — opens with WSA in 2026. Mamre/Devonshire Rd interchange enables industrial growth.
analogue: WestConnex stages — generally on schedule, modest overruns. M12 simpler greenfield project.

name: Mamre Road Stage 2 Upgrade
desc: $1B upgrade of Mamre Rd between Erskine Park and Kemps Creek. 4-lane divided highway. Critical industrial access road — Mamre Industrial Estate is fastest-growing logistics hub in Australia. 2025-26 NSW Budget funded.
analogue: Princes Hwy upgrades — typically on schedule for state-funded road works.

name: Elizabeth Drive Upgrade — Aerotropolis Access
desc: $800M upgrade to connect new airport. Currently 2-lane country road, will be widened to 4 lanes with bridges over major creeks. Connects Bradfield to airport terminal area. Funded jointly with federal contribution.
analogue: Northern Road upgrade for WSA — delivered in stages, mostly on schedule.

name: 15th Avenue Transit Corridor — Liverpool to WSA
desc: East-west bus rapid transit linking Liverpool CBD to Western Sydney Airport via 15th Avenue. Initial planning funded; full corridor not yet. Critical for airport workforce from south-west Sydney. Western Sydney Leadership Dialogue priority advocacy item.
analogue: Brisbane busways — successful BRT model when fully separated; mixed-traffic versions perform poorly.

name: Henry Lawson Drive Upgrade — Stage 1A & 1B
desc: $220M jointly funded upgrade between Liverpool and Bankstown. Removes bottleneck on Georges River corridor. South-west Sydney truck route — supports container freight + airport access.
analogue: Standard road widening — low risk profile.

name: Bankstown Hospital Redevelopment
desc: $2B new Bankstown Hospital — replaces existing aged facility. ~500 beds. Anchor for Canterbury-Bankstown LGA (Australia's largest by population). Opens 2027.
analogue: NSW hospital builds typically run 10-15% over budget, on schedule.

name: Rouse Hill Hospital
desc: New hospital for Sydney's North-West growth area — Box Hill / Rouse Hill / Marsden Park. ~300 beds, full maternity suite (federal $90M maternity supplement 2025-26 budget). Long-term health anchor for fastest-growing NSW corridor.
analogue: Northern Beaches Hospital (PPP) — controversial delivery model. Rouse Hill is direct state delivery.

name: Children's Hospital at Westmead — Stage 2
desc: Major upgrade to The Children's Hospital at Westmead — paediatric oncology, surgery, intensive care expansion. New 12-bed paediatric palliative care hospice (Mounties Care Cottage) co-located.
analogue: Existing Westmead campus — construction logistics complex but well-managed historically.

name: Upper South Creek Networks — Sydney Water
desc: Critical wastewater + recycled water network for Aerotropolis growth area. Treats and recycles water for industrial and residential use across Bradfield, Aerotropolis, Wilton SAP. Without it, no growth-area dwelling targets are achievable. $34B 10-year Sydney Water capital program — this is the largest Western Sydney project.
analogue: Sydney Water major projects — historically on schedule, budget pressure from material costs.

name: Aerotropolis Stormwater & Recycled Water
desc: Stormwater management infrastructure for Aerotropolis growth area. Includes flood mitigation, water-sensitive urban design, and integration with recycled water network from Upper South Creek.
analogue: Greenfield stormwater — typically on schedule, less risk than retrofit.

name: Central-West Orana REZ — Transmission
desc: 4.5 GW network capacity from 2028 — transmission backbone for first NSW Renewable Energy Zone. 7.15 GW of wind/solar/battery awarded Access Rights — enough to power 2.7 million homes. Early works started Feb 2025. Critical for state's 2030 emissions targets.
analogue: Project EnergyConnect (NSW-SA) — 6 months late, ~10% over budget. Comparable scale.

name: Powerhouse Parramatta
desc: Largest cultural investment since Sydney Opera House. First state cultural institution in Western Sydney. Opens late 2026. Replaces Ultimo Powerhouse (which gets converted to applied arts/sciences program). Major draw for Parramatta CBD activation.
analogue: Sydney Modern (Art Gallery NSW expansion) — opened 2022, 14% over budget. Powerhouse Parramatta has had higher overruns from design changes.

name: Penrith Stadium Redevelopment
desc: $309M redevelopment of BlueBet Stadium / Panthers home ground. ~25,000 capacity. Supports growing Western Sydney population and NRL/A-League content. Co-funded by Penrith Panthers and NRL.
analogue: Stadium NSW pipeline — Sydney Football Stadium (Allianz) on schedule and budget. Penrith similar profile.
